:::::::::::: formula-card
### Linear Equations — Solving Strategies {.unnumbered}

#### The Balance Method {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-yellow
**💡 Golden Rule** Whatever you do to one side, you **must** do to the other side.
:::

::: rule-group
**One-step**
$$x + 5 = 12 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 12 - 5 = 7$$
$$3x = 21 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 21 \div 3 = 7$$

**Two-step** — undo addition/subtraction *first*, then multiplication/division
$$2x + 3 = 11 \;\Rightarrow\; 2x = 8 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 4$$
:::

#### Unknowns on Both Sides {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-blue
**🎯 Strategy** Move all $x$ terms to one side, all numbers to the other.
:::

::: rule-group
**Method**:
$$5x + 2 = 2x + 8$$
$$5x - 2x = 8 - 2$$
$$3x = 6 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 2$$
:::

#### Expanding Brackets {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-pink
**⚠️ Remember** The number outside multiplies **every** term inside the bracket.
:::

::: rule-group
**Single bracket**:
$$3(2x + 5) = 27 \;\Rightarrow\; 6x + 15 = 27 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 2$$

**Two brackets**:
$$2(x + 3) + 4(x - 1) = 18$$
$$2x + 6 + 4x - 4 = 18$$
$$6x + 2 = 18 \;\Rightarrow\; x = \dfrac{8}{3}$$
:::

#### Algebraic Fractions {.unnumbered}

::: sticky-note-orange
**📝 Key Move** Multiply every term by the denominator (or LCM) to clear fractions first.
:::

::: rule-group
**Single fraction**:
$$\dfrac{2x + 1}{3} = 5 \;\Rightarrow\; 2x + 1 = 15 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 7$$

**Fractions on both sides — cross multiply**:
$$\dfrac{x + 1}{2} = \dfrac{x + 3}{4} \;\Rightarrow\; 4(x+1) = 2(x+3) \;\Rightarrow\; x = 1$$

**Adding fractions — use LCM**:
$$\dfrac{x}{2} + \dfrac{x}{3} = 5 \;\xrightarrow{\times 6}\; 3x + 2x = 30 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 6$$
:::

#### Forming Equations from Words {.unnumbered}

::: rule-group
**Step-by-step**:

1. Define your variable — *let $x = \ldots$*
2. Translate each condition into algebra
3. Form the equation
4. Solve and check your answer makes sense

**Example**: Three consecutive integers sum to 51.
$$x + (x+1) + (x+2) = 51 \;\Rightarrow\; 3x + 3 = 51 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 16$$
Numbers: 16, 17, 18. ✓
:::
::::::::::::
